mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS momentum signals expanding labor income and stronger expected corporate revenues, so higher recent payroll growth produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS 3-month change has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window or positive PAYEMS momentum is followed by negative returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS crossing above the zero-bound range marks the start of policy normalization, raising discount rates from very low levels and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% after being at or below 0.25% within the prior 6 months
regime_hint: early monetary tightening cycle after zero-rate policy
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is positive while FEDFUNDS remains below 1%, nominal revenue growth rises faster than discount rates, so equities earn positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change exceeds 2% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS trigger has a 60-day hit rate at or below 50% or if its average 60-day return is not higher than periods with FEDFUNDS above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: A falling UNRATE combined with positive PAYEMS growth confirms labor-market healing, lowering recession risk and supporting household demand, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 5.5% while PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered cases have median 60-day S&P 500 returns at or below zero or if the interaction term between UNRATE decline and PAYEMS growth has a nonpositive coefficient in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Negative INDPRO momentum signals weakening factory output and lower operating leverage for cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or if the triggered mean return is statistically indistinguishable from non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in HOUST leads weaker construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit-sensitive household spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 6 months or crosses below 1000
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside triggers have positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if their average return is not lower than the unconditional 60-day average.

mechanism_narrative: Higher RSAFS growth indicates stronger consumer spending and near-term sales growth for listed firms, so positive retail-sales momentum produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS 1-month or 3-month growth has Rank IC below 0.02 with 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window or positive RSAFS momentum fails to outperform negative momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation momentum is high and UNRATE is at or above 5%, the economy exhibits stagflation pressure that raises policy-risk premia and lowers expected margins, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change exceeds 3% and UNRATE is at or above 5.0%
regime_hint: stagflation-risk regime with labor slack and rising prices
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation-risk observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if adding the CPIAUCSL-by-UNRATE interaction does not improve walk-forward classification accuracy.
